item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Part of the universe was pocket-universed
what is left of the foundation in there only notices the apple-pie-human that was left.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i've come back to this one, months later, to change my novote to a downvote. i fully expected to upvote this one, but the more i went through the logs, the more i remembered why i didn't like it in the first place. too weirdly random without justification. sorry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: probably coincidence, but remind me of SCP-3049.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is the fault of a reality warpers subconscious? At least, that’s the vibe I’m getting from it. Very weird, and very well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this because there's a lot of a "WHAT DID THAT GUY DO" vibe to it. (bing crosby and carl sagan don't exist in this universe, and i feel like that's his fault.)

i don't like this because it's random, and all over the place, and there really isn't enough meat to the article to justify the randomness.

overall this plays like a too-short and and jokey version of 3812. now, there was a lot of narrative weirdness to 3812 that made sense in-universe, but this one doesn't even feel like it makes sense in baseline SCP. i'm novoting instead of downvoting, though, because there are still things i really like about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this one, intriguing enough and still left a lot to think.
It would be even better if I didn’t read SCP-3049 first.;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely delightful. I love a good Carl Sagan bit.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Apple pie from scratch, etc. Process Church shoutout! Absolute insanity in the space of 1 hour.

My understanding: A reality bender was watching Cosmos, and thought "I should make myself an apple pie from scratch." This SCP was written by the Foundation within the universe that was created in order to make that apple pie.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This absolutely insane and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this reminds me a lot of the tv show universes in SCP-2614, where fictional universes within fictional universes makes less and less sense. if i understand this correctly, this universe was created just to make an apple pie so it cuts a lot of corners in logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like some kind of reality bent, universal love story just happened. That shit was bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a bizarre delight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really hope this gets a declassified entry at some point, because I do not understand it. Same goes for a lot of the recent SCPs, too :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I might understand it, but I probably don't. I've read it four times and still have a hard time understanding what's going on. A definite upvote for making my brain overheat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I do a little too much editing and writing at 1am. I've been skimming it to see if there is a unnecessary or odd phrase here or there that I've missed in my recent edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No-vote from me. Dont get me wrong, this is a nice piece of writing, but it just feels a tad bit too random.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writing wise, this has a lot of clunky sentence structure, breaking the flow and leaving it far rougher to read than it should be. Content wise, this is fun. Solid weirdness paired with an underlying narrative which isn't too hard to piece together from the details provided (while not falling into the crutch of being "media reference anomaly" with no other substance).

+1

item.title: Re: I am incredibly confused
item.content:encoded: I was getting SCP-4231 vibes, mostly because of the mention of Cornwall as well as the human-made Scranton reality anchors. 4231 also highlighted Hume levels a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wild. My gears started turning as soon as the yachts circumnavigated the globe in 20ish minutes. Brilliant. +1

item.title: I am incredibly confused
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this is partially a reference to something. Or maybe I'm just stupid. It did sort of remind me of that one episode of Star Trek TNG where Beverly Crusher is trapped in an alternate universe where the universe itself keeps getting smaller and people are disappearing, and only she realizes anything is changing.

Aside from that, it's well written as it is. As I said, I don't really get it, so if anyone could illuminate me on what exactly is going on here I'd greatly appreciate it. Since I don't like to cast judgement on things I already admit to not understanding, I don't feel like I can rate this yet. Though I have a feeling I'll probably give it an upvote in the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I removed one of the "hume" instances in the note for the table. I'll take a comb through it tonight to see if I can grab a few more.

Edit: Now there are only two mentions of the word hume and a number of ultimately redundant or unnecessary phrases removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wish I could have sunk my teeth into this one during crit, pun intended. There's a lot of lines that could use some tightening, and you hume say hume the hume word hume a lot. The actual meat of this article though is exactly my cup of tea: reality benders turning the world into a manic pixie dreamscape from their repressed psyche. Love it, and you executed it well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
haha, what?
old man turns into apple pie, more at 11

well, you know what they say
"If you wish to make an apple pie from scratch, you must first invent the universe." - Carl Sagan

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Country is omitted intentionally and not due to data redaction protocols. The area this setting was created to resemble is Cornwall, England.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of these locations would be a lot easier to parse if countries were included. Which Truro is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. Not much more to say; it's a beautifully subtle and creative effect, to a degree that I don't often see on the site. Solid upvote.

item.title: Author corner post for a barbed wire fence
item.content:encoded:
Image attribution:
CC BY-SA 4.0
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Golden_Apple_Pie.jpg

Critters:
MaliceAforethought
Crashington
Lord_of_Laugh
I forgot most of you, my apologies there. Feel free to send me a poke if you did a beta read of this.
